cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user is asking about what we know about garage sales based on the provided passage. The options are A to D. Let me go through the text again to make sure I don't miss any details. The passage mentions that weekend outdoor garage sales have become very much a part of American culture. So that supports option C, which states they are popular with Americans. The passage also says they are usually held in spring and fall when the weather is good. But option A says "only held in the spring," which is incorrect because the text mentions spring and fall. So A is out. Option B claims the goods are totally useless, but the text says the homeowner is clearing out items that are no longer useful to him, not that they're useless. The items are in good condition and can be great buys if you search carefully. So B is wrong. Option D says all the goods are sold far below their real value. The passage mentions that if the owner is selling their home, they might have to practically give things away at prices below true worth.
